email.description: Email address of the requester. If no contact exists with this email address in Freshdesk, it will be added as a new contact.

facebookId.description: Facebook ID of the requester. If no contact exists with this facebook_id, then a new contact will be created.

phone.description: Phone number of the requester. If no contact exists with this phone number in Freshdesk, it will be added as a new contact. If the phone number is set and the email address is not, then the name attribute is mandatory.

requesterId.description: User ID of the requester. For existing contacts, the requester_id can be passed instead of the requester's email.

twitterId.description: Twitter handle of the requester. If no contact exists with this handle in Freshdesk, it will be added as a new contact.

uniqueExternalId.description: External ID of the requester. If no contact exists with this external ID in Freshdesk, they will be added as a new contact.

source.description: The channel through which the ticket was created

agent.description: ID of the agent to whom the ticket has been assigned. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

ccEmails.description: Separated by a comma (,) email addresses added in the 'cc' field of the incoming ticket email

company.description: Company ID of the requester. This attribute can only be set if the Multiple Companies feature is enabled (Estate plan and above). Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

dueBy.description: Timestamp that denotes when the ticket is due to be resolved

emailConfigId.description: ID of email config which is used for this ticket. (i.e., support@yourcompany.com/sales@yourcompany.com) If product_id is given and email_config_id is not given, product's primary email_config_id will be set.

frDueBy.description: Timestamp that denotes when the first response is due

group.description: ID of the group to which the ticket has been assigned. The default value is the ID of the group that is associated with the given email_config_id. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

product.description: ID of the product to which the ticket is associated. It will be ignored if the email_config_id attribute is set in the request. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

tags.description: Separated by a comma (,) tags that have been associated with the ticket

type.description: Helps categorize the ticket according to the different kinds of issues your support team deals with

order.description: Order sort attribute ascending or descending

agent.description: ID of the agent to whom the ticket has been assigned. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

ccEmails.description: Separated by a comma (,) email addresses added in the 'cc' field of the incoming ticket email

company.description: Company ID of the requester. This attribute can only be set if the Multiple Companies feature is enabled (Estate plan and above). Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

dueBy.description: Timestamp that denotes when the ticket is due to be resolved

emailConfigId.description: ID of email config which is used for this ticket. (i.e., support@yourcompany.com/sales@yourcompany.com) If product_id is given and email_config_id is not given, product's primary email_config_id will be set.

frDueBy.description: Timestamp that denotes when the first response is due

group.description: ID of the group to which the ticket has been assigned. The default value is the ID of the group that is associated with the given email_config_id. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

product.description: ID of the product to which the ticket is associated. It will be ignored if the email_config_id attribute is set in the request. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

email.description: Email address of the requester. If no contact exists with this email address in Freshdesk, it will be added as a new contact.

facebookId.description: Facebook ID of the requester. If no contact exists with this facebook_id, then a new contact will be created.

phone.description: Phone number of the requester. If no contact exists with this phone number in Freshdesk, it will be added as a new contact. If the phone number is set and the email address is not, then the name attribute is mandatory.

requesterId.description: User ID of the requester. For existing contacts, the requester_id can be passed instead of the requester's email.

twitterId.description: Twitter handle of the requester. If no contact exists with this handle in Freshdesk, it will be added as a new contact.

uniqueExternalId.description: External ID of the requester. If no contact exists with this external ID in Freshdesk, they will be added as a new contact.

source.description: The channel through which the ticket was created

tags.description: Separated by a comma (,) tags that have been associated with the ticket

type.description: Helps categorize the ticket according to the different kinds of issues your support team deals with

returnAll.description: Whether to return all results or only up to a given limit

limit.description: Max number of results to return

email.description: Primary email address of the contact. If you want to associate additional email(s) with this contact, use the other_emails attribute.

company_id.description: ID of the primary company to which this contact belongs

customFields.description: Key value pairs containing the name and value of the custom field. Only dates in the format YYYY-MM-DD are accepted as input for custom date fields.

description.description: A small description of the contact

email.description: Primary email address of the contact. If you want to associate additional email(s) with this contact, use the other_emails attribute.

language.description: Language of the contact. Default language is "en". This attribute can only be set if the Multiple Language feature is enabled (Garden plan and above).

other_companies.description: Additional companies associated with the contact. This attribute can only be set if the Multiple Companies feature is enabled (Estate plan and above).

other_emails.description: Additional emails associated with the contact

time_zone.description: Time zone of the contact. Default value is the time zone of the domain. This attribute can only be set if the Multiple Time Zone feature is enabled (Garden plan and above).

view_all_tickets.description: Whether the contact can see all the tickets that are associated with the company to which they belong
